title: Hawa Mahal
tagline: Hawa Mahal — heritage city of Rajasthan

overview.short: The Hawa Mahal is a palace in the city of Jaipur, Rajasthan, India.
overview.description: The Hawa Mahal is a palace in the city of Jaipur, Rajasthan, India. Built from red and pink sandstone, it is on the edge of the City Palace, Jaipur, and extends to the Zenana, or women's chambers. Hawa Mahal, also known as the "Palace of Winds" was built in 1799 by Maharaja Sawai Pratap Singh. The palace is known for its honeycomb-like façade, featuring numerous small windows and balconies.

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Badi Chaupar
topPlaces.description: Badi Chaupar (literally "Large Square"), also known as Manak Chowk, is a public square in Jaipur, India. It is one of the two major squares constructed by the King of Amer.

topPlaces.name: Jantar Mantar
topPlaces.description: A Jantar Mantar (जंतर मंतर, Hindustani pronunciation: [d͡ʒən̪t̪ər mən̪t̪ər] ‘calculation instruments’) is an assembly of stone-built astronomical instruments, designed to be used with the naked eye. There were five Jantar Mantars in India, built between 1724 and 1735.

topPlaces.name: Jantar Mantar, Jaipur
topPlaces.description: The Jantar Mantar is a collection of 19 astronomical instruments built by the Rajput king Sawai Jai Singh, the founder of Jaipur, Rajasthan. The monument was completed in 1734.

topPlaces.name: Kalki Mandir
topPlaces.description: Kalki Mandir (Sanskrit: कल्कि मंदिर) is a Hindu temple in Jaipur, Rajasthan, India, which was built by Jai Singh II in the 18th century. The temple is located in Sireh Deori Bazar opposite the palace gate.

topPlaces.name: City Palace, Jaipur
topPlaces.description: The City Palace, Jaipur is a royal residence and former administrative headquarters of the rulers of the Jaipur State in Jaipur, Rajasthan. Construction started soon after the establishment of the city of Jaipur under the reign of Maharaja Sawai Jai Singh II, who moved his court to Jaipur from Amber, in 1727.

topPlaces.name: Jaipur bombings
topPlaces.description: The Jaipur bombings were a series of nine synchronized bomb blasts that took place on 13 May 2008 within a span of 15 minutes at locations in Jaipur, the capital city of the Indian state of Rajasthan and a tourist destination. Official reports confirmed that 63 died while 216 or more people were injured.

topPlaces.name: Dhundhar
topPlaces.description: Dhundhar (Hindi: ḍhūnḍhāṛ, pronounced [ɖʱũːɖʱaːɽ]), also known as Jaipur region, is a historical region of Rajasthan state in western India. It includes the districts of Jaipur, Jamwaramgadh, Alwar, Neem ka Thana, Sikar, Jhunjhunu, Shekhawati lying to the east of the Aravalli Range, Dausa, Sawai Madhopur, Tonk, the southern part of Kotputli and the northern part of Karauli District.

topPlaces.name: Tarkeshwar Nath Temple
topPlaces.description: Tadkeshwar Nath Mahadev Temple (also known as Tadkeshwar Mahadev) is an ancient Hindu temple dedicated to Lord Shiva, located in the Jaipur district of Rajasthan, India. The temple is situated in the Choura Rasta area of Jaipur.

items.note: Badi Chaupar (literally "Large Square"), also known as Manak Chowk, is a public square in Jaipur, India. It is one of the two major squares constructed by the King of Amer.

A Jantar Mantar (जंतर मंतर, Hindustani pronunciation: [d͡ʒən̪t̪ər mən̪t̪ər] ‘calculation instruments’) is an assembly of stone-built astronomical instruments, designed to be used with the naked eye. There were five Jantar Mantars in India, built between 1724 and 1735.
The Jantar Mantar is a collection of 19 astronomical instruments built by the Rajput king Sawai Jai Singh, the founder of Jaipur, Rajasthan. The monument was completed in 1734.

Kalki Mandir (Sanskrit: कल्कि मंदिर) is a Hindu temple in Jaipur, Rajasthan, India, which was built by Jai Singh II in the 18th century. The temple is located in Sireh Deori Bazar opposite the palace gate.

The City Palace, Jaipur is a royal residence and former administrative headquarters of the rulers of the Jaipur State in Jaipur, Rajasthan. Construction started soon after the establishment of the city of Jaipur under the reign of Maharaja Sawai Jai Singh II, who moved his court to Jaipur from Amber, in 1727.

The Jaipur bombings were a series of nine synchronized bomb blasts that took place on 13 May 2008 within a span of 15 minutes at locations in Jaipur, the capital city of the Indian state of Rajasthan and a tourist destination. Official reports confirmed that 63 died while 216 or more people were injured.

Dhundhar (Hindi: ḍhūnḍhāṛ, pronounced [ɖʱũːɖʱaːɽ]), also known as Jaipur region, is a historical region of Rajasthan state in western India. It includes the districts of Jaipur, Jamwaramgadh, Alwar, Neem ka Thana, Sikar, Jhunjhunu, Shekhawati lying to the east of the Aravalli Range, Dausa, Sawai Madhopur, Tonk, the southern part of Kotputli and the northern part of Karauli District.

Tadkeshwar Nath Mahadev Temple (also known as Tadkeshwar Mahadev) is an ancient Hindu temple dedicated to Lord Shiva, located in the Jaipur district of Rajasthan, India. The temple is situated in the Choura Rasta area of Jaipur.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Hawa Mahal?
faq.a: The best time to visit Hawa Mahal is Oct – Mar. Summers are 28–44°C and winters are 8–25°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Hawa Mahal?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Jaipur International Airport (11 km) and the nearest railway station is Jaipur Junction (4 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Hawa Mahal?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Hawa Mahal at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Hawa Mahal?
faq.a: Stays in Hawa Mahal start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Hawa Mahal?
faq.a: The top attractions are Badi Chaupar, Jantar Mantar, Jantar Mantar, Jaipur, Kalki Mandir, City Palace, Jaipur.
